expected_result: Identify exposed services even when the visible entry point has been redirected or mirrored.
tutorial: 1. Ask uke for the target IP address. 2. Run nmap against the target. 3. Compare discovered ports with expected defaults. 4. Test the unusual port with the appropriate client. 5. Confirm whether the service is mirrored or redirected.
mitre_references: ATT&CK T1046 Network Service Discovery; D3FEND D3-CAA Connection Attempt Analysis
judo_references: Mikiri through reading the opponent before commitment; tsukuri by preparing the right line of attack
karate_references: Mikiri; choosing the right distance and timing before entry
jujitsu_references: Reading the partner's structure before applying the technique

expected_result: Isolate one hostile signal from noisy system logs.
tutorial: 1. Start live monitoring with tail -f on syslog or journal output. 2. Observe one raw connection event. 3. Add grep filtering by source IP, port, or keyword. 4. Repeat the hostile action. 5. Confirm that only the relevant trace remains visible.
mitre_references: D3FEND D3-CAA Connection Attempt Analysis; D3FEND D3-ANAA Administrative Network Activity Analysis
judo_references: Kuzushi through information control; preparing correct reaction
karate_references: Mikiri and sen-no-sen through early recognition
jujitsu_references: Situational reading before response

expected_result: Locate the active remote terminal session and identify the corresponding local process.
tutorial: 1. Wait for uke to open a remote session. 2. Run w to identify the active TTY. 3. Run ps -fat to map the TTY to a PID. 4. State which process controls the session. 5. Verify by observing session behavior.
mitre_references: D3FEND D3-RTSD Remote Terminal Session Detection; D3FEND D3-PT Process Termination
judo_references: Go no sen: answer after the opponent has committed
karate_references: Responsive timing after the attack has become visible
jujitsu_references: Receiving then identifying the line of attack before countering

expected_result: Terminate an unauthorized remote session cleanly and decisively.
tutorial: 1. Detect the incoming session. 2. Identify the responsible PID with w and ps. 3. Kill the correct process. 4. Confirm the remote disconnect. 5. Reset the system for the next repetition.
mitre_references: D3FEND D3-PT Process Termination; D3FEND D3-RTSD Remote Terminal Session Detection
judo_references: Kake as completion after preparation and kuzushi
karate_references: Decisive finishing after correct timing
jujitsu_references: Completion of a counter-control sequence

expected_result: Interrupt a live network connection and reverse initiative.
tutorial: 1. Observe the active session with ss or watch ss. 2. Identify the destination port in use. 3. Run tcpkill on the active port. 4. Wait for uke to lose the session. 5. Use the opening to reconnect or reconfigure.
mitre_references: D3FEND D3-ANAA Administrative Network Activity Analysis; D3FEND D3-PT Process Termination
judo_references: Kaeshi through immediate reversal after receiving pressure
karate_references: Countering after the opponent extends into range
jujitsu_references: Reversal of the attacker's commitment

expected_result: Break the attacker's assumptions by changing the visible service posture without losing controlled access.
tutorial: 1. Expose one known service. 2. Let uke discover and begin to target it. 3. Change or redirect the exposed port. 4. Restart the service or apply redirection. 5. Force uke to re-read the environment.
mitre_references: ATT&CK T1046 Network Service Discovery; D3FEND D3-ANAA Administrative Network Activity Analysis
judo_references: Kuzushi by breaking posture and expectation
karate_references: Breaking structure before the decisive action
jujitsu_references: Destroying the partner's prepared line before execution

expected_result: Create a convincing but limited lure that forces the partner to verify instead of assuming.
tutorial: 1. Start a netcat listener on a chosen port. 2. Let uke discover the open port. 3. Let uke connect with a client. 4. Return limited or ambiguous data. 5. Debrief what evidence was real and what remained uncertain.
mitre_references: ATT&CK T1046 Network Service Discovery; CAPEC-624 Fake Services
judo_references: Use of invitation and false opening to manipulate reaction
karate_references: Kyojitsu style guarded and unguarded contrast
jujitsu_references: Using an apparent opening to draw commitment

expected_result: Build a controlled sequence of repeated access attempts across discovered services.
tutorial: 1. Discover the target services. 2. Choose one known credential pair. 3. Attempt access over ssh, telnet, or ftp in sequence. 4. Observe which service accepts the entry. 5. Stop once one foothold is established.
mitre_references: ATT&CK T1110.004 Credential Stuffing; ATT&CK T1046 Network Service Discovery
judo_references: Tsukuri by constructing opportunity across repeated entries
karate_references: Building the right conditions before the decisive technique
jujitsu_references: Progressive setup across multiple lines of attack

expected_result: Use one host as an intermediate point to access a second host and alter attribution.
tutorial: 1. Access uke1 through a remote session. 2. From uke1, open a second session to uke2. 3. Observe that uke2 sees uke1 as the source. 4. Maintain control long enough to demonstrate the pivot. 5. Disconnect and debrief the path.
mitre_references: ATT&CK T1563.001 Remote Service Session Hijacking; ATT&CK T1021 Remote Services
judo_references: Transition from first grip to second advantage
karate_references: Changing angle and line after initial entry
jujitsu_references: Flowing from one engagement to another

expected_result: Identify the target service by recognizing the attack pattern during the earliest connection attempts.
tutorial: 1. Uke scans or probes ports one by one. 2. Tori watches live logs or socket state. 3. Tori calls out the likely target port before the final connection succeeds. 4. Confirm the guess. 5. Repeat with different ports.
mitre_references: D3FEND D3-CAA Connection Attempt Analysis; D3FEND D3-ANAA Administrative Network Activity Analysis
judo_references: Initiative through reading the opponent's preparation
karate_references: Sen-no-sen through intercepting intention
jujitsu_references: Reading intent before full commitment

expected_result: Observe commands executed inside an active session without taking it over.
tutorial: 1. Detect the remote session. 2. Identify the controlling PID. 3. Use peekfd or equivalent observation tooling. 4. Watch stdin, stdout, and stderr. 5. Report the activity without disrupting it.
mitre_references: D3FEND D3-RTSD Remote Terminal Session Detection; D3FEND D3-ANAA Administrative Network Activity Analysis
judo_references: Observation before action; keeping balance without overcommitting
karate_references: Mikiri through detailed reading of movement
jujitsu_references: Studying intent through the partner's action stream

expected_result: Gain access to uke's host and remove uke's local foothold as a decisive counter.
tutorial: 1. Establish a remote session to uke. 2. Run w to locate the local TTY without a remote IP. 3. Run ps -fat to identify the local controlling PID. 4. Kill the local session process. 5. Confirm that uke is ejected from the host.
mitre_references: ATT&CK T1059 Command and Scripting Interpreter; D3FEND D3-PT Process Termination
judo_references: Kaeshi and kake after entering past the guard
karate_references: Decisive finish after gaining superior position
jujitsu_references: Completion of control once access is secured

expected_result: Recover and reuse recent command knowledge quickly under time pressure.
tutorial: 1. Start from a shell with prior activity. 2. Use Ctrl+r, !!, !N, or !-N to recall commands. 3. Re-execute the correct prior command. 4. State why the recalled sequence was chosen. 5. Repeat until recall becomes automatic.
mitre_references: ATT&CK T1059 Command and Scripting Interpreter
judo_references: Tsune as habit and constant practice
karate_references: Repetition until action becomes natural
jujitsu_references: Embedding technical habit through repeated form

expected_result: Confirm the content of a target file with the simplest correct command.
tutorial: 1. Receive a path from sensei or uke. 2. Read the file with cat, less, or more. 3. Identify the expected keyword or configuration value. 4. State the relevant finding aloud. 5. Close the reading tool cleanly.
mitre_references: ATT&CK T1005 Data from Local System; T1059 Command and Scripting Interpreter
judo_references: Correct reading before action
karate_references: No wasted movement; direct path to the information
jujitsu_references: Situational confirmation before execution

expected_result: Modify one service configuration to create a new controlled opening.
tutorial: 1. Identify the current service configuration file. 2. Read the existing port setting. 3. Change the port with sed or a controlled editor. 4. Restart the service. 5. Verify the new port with ss or nmap.
mitre_references: ATT&CK T1562 Impair Defenses; ATT&CK T1574 Hijack Execution Flow; D3FEND D3-ANAA Administrative Network Activity Analysis
judo_references: Tsukuri by building the future state before use
karate_references: Preparation and positioning before attack or defense
jujitsu_references: Constructing the condition that makes the next move possible

expected_result: Prepare and complete a reverse shell exchange when uke calls back to tori.
tutorial: 1. Start a listening socket on tori. 2. Have uke launch a reverse shell toward the listener. 3. Confirm that the callback arrives. 4. Execute one verification command. 5. Close the shell cleanly.
mitre_references: ATT&CK T1059 Command and Scripting Interpreter; ATT&CK T1105 Ingress Tool Transfer
judo_references: Kake as committed completion after setup
karate_references: Finishing the technique once the opening exists
jujitsu_references: Full execution after the opening has been created

expected_result: Overwhelm the partner's certainty by applying simultaneous or rapid-sequence pressure across several candidate ports.
tutorial: 1. Discover the partner's service surface. 2. Open or attempt multiple connections in quick succession. 3. Observe which line forces defensive reaction first. 4. Shift to the most vulnerable port. 5. Confirm whether the pressure changed uke's configuration or timing.
mitre_references: ATT&CK T1046 Network Service Discovery; ATT&CK T1021 Remote Services
judo_references: Superior initiative by acting before the opponent settles
karate_references: Taking initiative before the opponent's structure is complete
jujitsu_references: Forcing reaction through early pressure

expected_result: Obtain just enough information with the smallest necessary command footprint.
tutorial: 1. Select the narrowest useful nmap or ss scope. 2. Run the scan against one host or one service range only. 3. Record the minimum sufficient information. 4. Stop once the objective is reached. 5. Explain why broader action was unnecessary.
mitre_references: ATT&CK T1046 Network Service Discovery
judo_references: Maximum efficiency with minimum effort
karate_references: Economy of motion and commitment
jujitsu_references: Using only the force necessary for the effect

expected_result: Intercept the opponent's first move by starting from a prepared shell state instead of reacting after delay.
tutorial: 1. Prepare the shell with the needed command partly typed or mentally rehearsed. 2. Wait for uke's signal or first visible action. 3. Complete and launch the defensive or investigative command immediately. 4. Compare the response time with an unprepared repetition. 5. Debrief what preparation reduced delay.
mitre_references: D3FEND D3-CAA Connection Attempt Analysis; D3FEND D3-RTSD Remote Terminal Session Detection
judo_references: Initiative begins in preparation before contact
karate_references: Acting at the instant the attack appears
jujitsu_references: Pre-contact readiness before the partner completes the attack

expected_result: Name the nature of an attack from its first visible packet or first log line before the session stabilizes.
tutorial: 1. Start tail -f on syslog or watch -n2 ss -tnp. 2. Have uke begin one attack pattern such as ssh, telnet, or scan. 3. Call out the likely protocol or intent from the earliest visible trace. 4. Confirm the guess after the connection develops. 5. Repeat until the naming becomes immediate.
mitre_references: D3FEND D3-CAA Connection Attempt Analysis; D3FEND D3-ANAA Administrative Network Activity Analysis
judo_references: Reading the opponent at the moment of engagement
karate_references: Interception through early perception
jujitsu_references: Identifying the attack as it starts rather than after it lands

expected_result: Prepare the correct log filter before the next attack so the hostile action is isolated at the moment it begins.
tutorial: 1. Observe one sample hostile action. 2. Build a grep filter from the source IP, port, or service trace. 3. Arm the filtered monitor before the next repetition. 4. Have uke repeat the same attack. 5. Confirm that the event is visible immediately with no extra search.
mitre_references: D3FEND D3-CAA Connection Attempt Analysis; D3FEND D3-ANAA Administrative Network Activity Analysis
judo_references: Preparation and timing joined into one motion
karate_references: Setting the line before the attack enters
jujitsu_references: Establishing the receiving structure before contact

expected_result: Deny the exact line of entry just before uke completes the intended connection.
tutorial: 1. Let uke reveal the intended target service through probing. 2. Read the likely destination port from logs or socket state. 3. Change, drop, or redirect that entry point before the final access attempt. 4. Observe the failed connection. 5. Reset and repeat with a different service.
mitre_references: ATT&CK T1046 Network Service Discovery; D3FEND D3-ANAA Administrative Network Activity Analysis; D3FEND D3-PT Process Termination
judo_references: Breaking the entry at the moment it forms
karate_references: Acting on the threshold of contact
jujitsu_references: Closing the opening as the partner commits to it

expected_result: Catch the opponent's predictable reconnection attempt and cut it at the moment it restarts.
tutorial: 1. Force uke's session to drop once. 2. Predict that uke will reconnect using the same service. 3. Watch the port and session state. 4. The moment the reconnection begins, terminate it again with the prepared command. 5. Confirm whether the second interruption was faster than the first.
mitre_references: D3FEND D3-RTSD Remote Terminal Session Detection; D3FEND D3-PT Process Termination
judo_references: Responding at the moment the renewed attack begins
karate_references: Countering the second entry before it settles
jujitsu_references: Meeting the repeated attack on its restart

expected_result: Identify the correct remote TTY before uke has time to establish a stable working session.
tutorial: 1. Start from an empty local state. 2. Have uke open a remote session and wait briefly before typing commands. 3. Run w immediately and call out the incoming TTY. 4. Confirm by having uke continue the session. 5. Repeat until the TTY is identified before meaningful activity begins.
mitre_references: D3FEND D3-RTSD Remote Terminal Session Detection
judo_references: Reading the line of action as it enters
karate_references: Recognizing the attack before it develops depth
jujitsu_references: Immediate identification before the partner settles into control
